test_cases.input: URL: https://www.city-planning-dept.example.gov/zoning/proposed-amendments. Context: City planning department posts proposed zoning amendments. A developer has applied to rezone agricultural land for a warehouse complex. Need to know immediately when the amendment language changes or new documents are added. Basic tech skills — comfortable with browser extensions but not scripts. Six-week monitoring period until council vote.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: No AI tells. Strongest craft: practical reasoning woven into setup instructions ('even a one-word change to amendment language matters here').

test_cases.input: URLs: 14 pharmaceutical company pages publishing clinical trial results. Context: Health reporter tracking whether drug companies retroactively amend reported outcomes. Need continuous monitoring for six months. Newsroom has a developer for technical setup. Hourly checks preferred.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: No lexical tells. Appropriate technical register for newsroom developer audience. Strongest craft: monitoring log adapted with Company/URL column for multi-page scenario.

test_cases.input: URL: https://regional-water-authority.example.org/compliance/violations. Context: Water-quality violations page where authority posts violations briefly then removes them. Freelance reporter, no tech support, no budget. Weekly checks fine — building a long-term database.
test_cases.editorial_naturalness_note: Strongest craft: proactive advice to manually archive weekly even without alerts, and monitoring log adapted with 'Still on page?' column reflecting the removal pattern. No tells.
